Thank you for coming, please, this lunch is my treat.
I would recommend the appetizers, they are the best part.
Let me recap the year before we order, it has been quite a journey.
My biggest takeaway is that I feel confident in English now.

Milton, it is so good to finally do this in person. A year ago I never imagined we would be celebrating like this.
Neither did I. Please, today I host, this lunch is my treat. I would recommend the appetizers here, they are the best part.
Then I trust your taste. Could you tell me what you are planning for next season?
Let me recap first. We are going to expand the collection and ship earlier. Honestly, my biggest takeaway this year is how confident I feel discussing all of this.
It shows. When that shipment was damaged, you told me you would fix it, and you did. That is why we are still here.
That means a lot. So let us raise a toast, to a great year, and to the ones ahead.
I would recommend the appetizers, and I am going to host us properly today.
She said my biggest takeaway was confidence, and I think she was right.
Could you tell me what you are planning for next season?
My biggest takeaway this year is that I feel confident.

Hi Milton, it is Karen. I am so glad we finally found a date for lunch. I booked us a table at that little Italian place near my office for one o'clock on Thursday. I thought it would be nice to celebrate properly, because honestly this past year working with you has been a real milestone for us. When you have a moment, let me know if there is anything you cannot eat, and I will tell the restaurant. Oh, and bring your appetite, because they say the appetizers there are the best part. I would love to just relax, catch up, and recap everything we have built together, no agenda, no slides. See you Thursday, and thank you again for everything. It really means a lot.

Hey, this is Milton, just a quick voice note to myself after lunch with Karen. A year ago I would have dreaded a meal like this in English. I would have rehearsed every line and still frozen. Today I hosted it. I recommended the appetizers, I raised a toast, and we talked for two hours without me reaching for Portuguese once. Karen told me she was impressed, and she said the whole partnership felt easier now that we understood each other so well. The biggest takeaway for me is confidence. I can solve a problem on the phone, ask an indirect question politely, talk about what we are going to do next quarter, and report a conversation back to my team, all without panic. If a client calls tomorrow with bad news, I know exactly what I would say. This was the milestone I needed. The English is not the goal anymore. It is just the tool, and finally it feels like mine.
